title: Bitfarms Reports Fourth Quarter 2024 Results
text: Bitfarms reported Q4 2024 results with total revenue of $56 million, up 21% year-over-year. Net income was $15 million or $0.03 per share, versus a $62 million net loss in Q4 2023. Adjusted EBITDA was $14 million (25% margin), down from $16 million (35% margin) in Q4 2023. Gross mining margin was 47%, declining from 57% in the prior year period. Direct cost per Bitcoin was $40,800 with total cash cost per Bitcoin of $60,800. Hashrate reached 18.6 EHuM, up 186% from Q4 2023, with current efficiency of 19 w/TH representing 45% improvement from Q4 2023. CEO Ben Gagnon stated the company transformed from international Bitcoin miner to North American energy and compute company. The company completed acquisition of Stronghold Digital Mining and sold 200 MW Yguazu Paraguay data center to HIVE Digital. Energy pipeline reached approximately 1.4 GW with approximately 80% US-based. The company projects no major miner purchases planned for 2025-2026, instead deploying capital toward U.S. energy infrastructure and HPC/AI development with capex requirements 20% lower than previously planned. Bitcoin earned in Q4 was 654 BTC with operating capacity of 394 MW.
